discrepancy: MD: '# SDTM v2.0 — Chapter 4: Associated Persons Data' vs PDF: '4  Associated Persons Data' (title format and numbering style differs, but semantic match)

MD metadata 'Source: SDTM v2.0, Section 4 (Page 50)' — MD-side document header added by extractor, not PDF verbatim. Fact is correct (PDF is page 50, SDTM v2.0) but this is editorial source attribution.
MD: '## Overview' is a synthetic section heading added by MD extractor. PDF has no '## Overview' heading; content follows directly after title.

MD: consolidated 3 sentences into 1 PARAGRAPH_INVALID_AGENT_ERROR. PDF atoms sv20_p0050_a002 (Associated persons are...), a003 (The structures of SDTM...), a004 (AP domains are created...) match verbatim when concatenated. MD agent incorrectly merged and tagged as PARAGRAPH.
MD: '### AP Domain Rules' is a synthetic subsection heading. PDF does not have this exact heading; instead PDF items a005-a007 are list items under the main text (no subheading in between).

MD: 'AP domains use the domain code prefix AP followed by the relevant domain code. For example, associated persons demographics would use APDM.' vs PDF a006: 'AP will be the prefix for the domain and dataset name, and will identify the dataset as AP data.' — semantically aligned (both state AP prefix rule) but PDF is more generic, MD adds example APDM.

MD: 'APID was removed from Section 3.1.4 (Identifiers for All Classes) because APID is only allowed in AP domains. It is still listed in Section 3 as an identifier.' vs PDF a007: 'The Associated Persons Identifier (APID) will be required in all AP datasets, and will identify records as AP data.' — MD includes editorial context about removal from Section 3.1.4 (fact-based but not PDF-stated), PDF states APID requirement. Both reference APID but different aspects; MD is synthesis + fact from PDF content.

MD a008 contains 3 linking variable definitions (RSUBJID, RDEVID, SREL spanning lines 15-18). PDF a012 + a013 cover: 'If the associated person is related to a study subject, RSUBJID identifies that subject; if the individual is related to a study device, RDEVID identifies the device.' + 'SREL describes the relationship of the associated person to a study subject, a study device, or the study.' MD expands with full definitions and null conditions from PDF table row a017-a019 content.
MD: '### Key Principles' is a synthetic section heading. PDF has no such section heading. MD organizes subsequent list items (a010-a013) under this synthetic category.

MD: 'AP domains conform to the same general observation class models (Interventions, Events, Findings) used for subject data' vs PDF a005: 'Implementers creating AP domains will follow the AP assumptions for the Identifier variables.' — Different focus (MD: class structure, PDF: identifier assumptions). Not direct match but both derive from PDF Section 4 content on AP conformance rules.

MD: 'The same variables, roles, and controlled terminology apply' vs PDF a009: 'Unless an exception is described in the SDTMIG-AP, all other general assumptions about SDTM and SDTMIG variables and domains apply to AP data.' — MD simplifies general conformance statement from PDF (both state AP adopts SDTM assumptions).

MD: 'Additional guidance for implementing AP domains is provided in the SDTM Implementation Guide: Associated Persons (SDTMIG-AP)' vs PDF a008: 'The SDTM Implementation Guide: Associated Persons (SDTMIG-AP; available at https://www.cdisc.org/standards/foundational/sdtmig/sdtmig-ap) provides implementation rules, advice, and examples.' — Both reference SDTMIG-AP, MD omits URL (reasonable simplification).

MD: 'AP domains are not part of the core SDTMIG for human clinical trials' — Not found verbatim in PDF atoms. This is a true statement (SDTMIG-AP is separate) but not directly stated in the pdf_v1.1 candidate set.
MD: '### Variables Used in Associated Persons Data' vs PDF a014: 'Associated Persons—Additional Identifier Variables' — Both are section headings for variable definitions, slight wording difference.

MD: 'The table of variables used in Associated Persons Data includes all variables from the general observation classes, with the addition of AP-specific identifiers:' vs PDF a010: 'The variables in the following table are used in AP domains.' — Both introduce the table; MD adds descriptive context about what table includes (general obs classes + AP-specific).

MD: markdown table header '| Variable | Label | Type | Role | Notes |' vs PDF a015 pipe-separated column headers '# | Variable Name | Variable Label | Type | Format | Role | Variable(s) Qualified | Usage Restrictions | Variable C-code | Definition | Notes | Examples' — MD is simplified subset (fewer columns). Both are table headers for variable data.

MD TABLE_ROW: 'APID | Associated Persons Identifier | Char | Identifier | Identifier for a single associated person...' vs PDF a016 TABLE_ROW: '1 | APID | Associated Persons Identifier | Char | | Identifier | ... | Identifier for a single associated person...' — Semantically identical content, MD simplified column structure (omits Format, C-code, Definition columns).

MD TABLE_ROW RSUBJID: 'Related Subject or Pool Identifier | Char | Identifier | Identifier for a related subject or pool...' vs PDF a017: '2 | RSUBJID | Related Subject or Pool Identifier | Char | | Identifier | ... | Identifier for a related subject or pool...' — Same semantic content, MD simplified columns.

MD TABLE_ROW RDEVID: 'Related Device Identifier | Char | Identifier | Identifier for a related device...' vs PDF a018: '3 | RDEVID | Related Device Identifier | Char | | Identifier | ... | Identifier for a related device...' — Semantically identical, MD column structure simplified.

MD TABLE_ROW SREL: 'Subject, Device, or Study Relationship | Char | Identifier | If RSUBJID is populated, describes the relationship to the subject or pool. If RDEVID is populated, describes the relationship to the device. If both are null, describes the relationship to the study.' vs PDF a019: '4 | SREL | Subject, Device, or Study Relationship | Char | | Identifier | ... | If RSUBJID is populated, describes the relationship of the associated person(s) identified in APID to the subject or pool identified in RSUBJID. If RDEVID is populated, describes the relationship of the associated person(s) identified in APID to the device identified in RDEVID. If RSUBJID and RDEVID are null, SREL describes the relationship of the associated person(s) identified in APID to the study identified in STUDYID.' — MD simplifies by removing 'identified in APID/RSUBJID/etc.' modifiers, but core logic identical.

MD PARAGRAPH_INVALID_AGENT_ERROR: 'Relationships of an associated person to study subjects may be represented in the Associated Persons Relationships dataset. When an individual has multiple collected relationships to a study subject or study subjects, those multiple relationships must be represented in that relationships dataset.' vs PDF a020+a021 (two SENTENCE atoms concatenated) — exact verbatim match across both PDF atoms. MD consolidated 2 sentences into 1 PARAGRAPH with invalid type tag.
